revision.text: ''宗像神社'''は[[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸 ]]に存在する神社である。 == 歴史 == [[876年]]([[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年)の創建だと伝わる。祭神は[[田心姫神]]などの三女神。 [[1604年]]、[[1831年]]に改築されている。 == 概要 == [[印旛沼]]

'''宗像神社'''は[[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸 ]]に存在する神社である。 == 歴史 == [[876年]]([[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年)の創建だと伝わる。祭神は[[田心姫神]]、[[市杵島姫神|市杵島姫命]]などの三女神。 [[1604年]]、[[1831年]]に改築されている。 == 概要 == [[印旛沼]]の北岸台地南側周辺の宗像神社の[[総鎮守]]といわれていて、当地周辺の宗像神社の中で一番最古の社だといわれている。 近隣には、この地に伝わる龍伝説に纏わる[[薬王寺 (白井市)|薬王寺]]や、[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]がある。 == 交通 == [[千葉ニュータウン中央駅]]から25分 == その他 == この項目のテンプレートをよろしくお願いします。

'''宗像神社'''(むなかたじんじゃ)は[[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸 ]]に存在する神社である。 == 歴史 == [[876年]]([[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年)の創建だと伝わる。祭神は[[田心姫神]]、[[市杵島姫神|市杵島姫命]]などの[[宗像三女神]]であり、 [[1604年]]、[[1831年]]に改築されている。 == 概要 == [[印旛沼]]の北岸台地南側周辺の宗像神社の[[総鎮守]]といわれていて、当地周辺の宗像神社の中で一番最古の社だといわれている。 近隣には、この地に伝わる龍伝説に纏わる[[薬王寺 (白井市)|薬王寺]]や、[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]がある。 == 交通 == [[千葉ニュータウン中央駅]]から25分 == その他 == この項目のテンプレートをよろしくお願いします。

'''宗像神社'''(むなかたじんじゃ)は[[千葉県]][[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸 ]]に存在する神社である。 == 歴史 == [[876年]]([[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年)の創建だと伝わる。祭神は[[田心姫神]]、[[市杵島姫神|市杵島姫命]]などの[[宗像三女神]]であり、 [[1604年]]、[[1831年]]に改築されている。 == 概要 == [[印旛沼]]の北岸台地南側周辺の宗像神社の[[総鎮守]]といわれていて、当地周辺の宗像神社の中で一番最古の社だといわれている。 近隣には、この地に伝わる龍伝説に纏わる[[薬王寺 (白井市)|薬王寺]]や、[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]がある。 == 交通 == [[千葉ニュータウン中央駅]]から25分 == その他 == この項目のテンプレートをよろしくお願いします。

'''宗像神社'''(むなかたじんじゃ)は[[千葉県]][[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸 ]]に存在する神社である。 == 祭神 == *[[宗像三女神]] **[[田心姫神]] **[[市杵島姫神]] == 歴史 == [[876年]]([[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年)の創建だと伝わる。[[1604年]]([[慶長]]8年)と[[1831年]]([[天保]]2年)に再建された。 == 概要 == [[印旛沼]]の北岸台地南側周辺の宗像神社の[[総鎮守]]といわれていて、当地周辺の宗像神社の中で一番最古の社だといわれている。 近隣には、この地に伝わる龍伝説に纏わる[[薬王寺 (白井市)|薬王寺]]や、[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]がある。 == 交通 == [[千葉ニュータウン中央駅]]から25分

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shiroi]] [[Category:Buildings in Shiroi]] [[Category:Munakata shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]
[[ja:宗像神社]]

== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Shiroi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Shiroi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Munakata shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:宗像神社]]

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.

== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604年}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831年}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604th year }} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831st year }} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876th year }} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604th year }} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831st year }} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876th year }} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604th year }} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831st year }} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft||fr|12=draft}} or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses|12=draft}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]] '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|ja|白井市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸|12=draft}} district.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=draft}} ** <!--does it worship Tagitsuhime-->
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|ja|876th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|ja|1604th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|ja|1831st year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpō era|ja|天保|12=draft}} 2).
[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]] == Overview == The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=draft}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=draft}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region.
Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|12=draft}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=draft}}.
== Access == Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|ja|千葉ニュータウン中央駅|12=draft}}.
{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Chiba Prefecture}}

10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宗像神社 (白井市) up to rev 64605345
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年8月4日 (月) 15:41 (UTC)}} [[ファイル:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|社殿]] '''宗像神社'''(むなかたじんじゃ)は[[千葉県]][[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸]]に存在する神社である。
== 祭神 == *[[宗像三女神]] **[[田心姫神]] **[[市杵島姫神]]
== 歴史 == [[876年]]([[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年)の創建だと伝わる。[[1604年]]([[慶長]]8年)と[[1831年]]([[天保]]2年)に再建された。
[[ファイル:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|鳥居]] == 概要 == [[印旛沼]]の北岸台地南側周辺に多くある宗像神社の[[総鎮守]]といわれていて、当地の宗像神社の中で最古の社だといわれている。 近隣には、この地に伝わる龍伝説に纏わる[[薬王寺 (白井市)|薬王寺]]や、[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]がある。
== 交通 == [[千葉ニュータウン中央駅]]から25分 {{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|宗像神社}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:むなかたしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:白井市の歴史]] [[Category:白井市の建築物]] [[Category:宗像神社|しろい]]

{{出典の明記|date=2014年8月4日 (月) 15:41 (UTC)}} [[ファイル:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|社殿]] '''宗像神社'''(むなかたじんじゃ)は[[千葉県]][[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸]]に存在する神社である。
== 祭神 == *[[宗像三女神]] **[[田心姫神]] **[[市杵島姫神]]
== 歴史 == [[876年]]([[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年)の創建だと伝わる。[[1604年]]([[慶長]]8年)と[[1831年]]([[天保]]2年)に再建された。
[[ファイル:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|鳥居]] == 概要 == [[印旛沼]]の北岸台地南側周辺に多くある宗像神社の[[総鎮守]]といわれていて、当地の宗像神社の中で最古の社だといわれている。 近隣には、この地に伝わる龍伝説に纏わる[[薬王寺 (白井市)|薬王寺]]や、[[弁財天 (白井市)|弁財天]]がある。
== 交通 == [[千葉ニュータウン中央駅]]から25分 {{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|宗像神社}}
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{デフォルトソート:むなかたしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:白井市の歴史]] [[Category:白井市の建築物]] [[Category:宗像神社|しろい]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Munakata-jinja (Chiba)]][[ja:宗像神社 (白井市)]]
{{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Shiroi|qid=Q519187}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.795471|N|140.08812|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18339542]]
Wikidata name: Munakata-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=Does this shrine worship {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タギツヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}}
 or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]]
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiroi|Shiroi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyoto|qq=|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} district.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
** 

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:876|876]] ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] 18). It was rebuilt in [[:en:1604|1604]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] 8) and again in [[:en:1831|1831]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

== Access ==
Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Chiba New Town Chūō Station|Chiba New Town Chūō Station]].

{{commonscat|Munakata-jinja (Chiba)|Munakata Shrine}}

 or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]]
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiroi|Shiroi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyoto|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} district.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
** 

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:876|876]] ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] 18). It was rebuilt in [[:en:1604|1604]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] 8) and again in [[:en:1831|1831]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

== Access ==
Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Chiba New Town Chūō Station|Chiba New Town Chūō Station]].

 or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]]
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiroi|Shiroi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyoto|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} district.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
** 

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:876|876]] ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] 18). It was rebuilt in [[:en:1604|1604]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] 8) and again in [[:en:1831|1831]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

== Access ==
Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Chiba New Town Chūō Station|Chiba New Town Chūō Station]].

The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:876|876]] ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] 18). It was rebuilt in [[:en:1604|1604]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] 8) and again in [[:en:1831|1831]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}}
 or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]]
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiroi|Shiroi]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyoto|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} district.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
** 

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:876|876]] ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] 18). It was rebuilt in [[:en:1604|1604]] ([[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] 8) and again in [[:en:1831|1831]] ([[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

== Access ==
Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Chiba New Town Chūō Station|Chiba New Town Chūō Station]].

** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
** 

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876}} ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|en|1604|WD=Q6738|lt=1604}} ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831}} ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era}} 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

== Access ==
Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|en|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|WD=Q855466|lt=Chiba New Town Chūō Station}}.

{{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Shiroi|WD=Q519187|1=Shiroi}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.795471|N|140.08812|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}


{{Citation needed|date=August 4, 2014 (Monday) 15:41 (UTC)}}
 or just the other two {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 17:37, 3 March 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Munakata-shrine-zoomout-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Shrine Hall]]
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|en|Shiroi|WD=Q519187|lt=Shiroi|1=Shiroi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyoto|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16279521}} district.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
** 

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876|1=876}} ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|en|1604|WD=Q6738|lt=1604|1=1604}} ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era|1=Keichō}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}} ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}} 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=Chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

== Access ==
Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|en|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|WD=Q855466|lt=Chiba New Town Chūō Station|1=Chiba New Town Chūō Station}}.

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata-jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiroi|en|Shiroi|qid=Q519187|lt=Shiroi|1=Shiroi}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kiyoto|ja|清戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyoto|1=Kiyodo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16279521}} district.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Three Munakata Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
** {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
** 

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|876|en|876|qid=Q23815|lt=876|1=876}} ({{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}} 18). It was rebuilt in {{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604|1=1604}} ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era|1=Keichō}} 8) and again in {{ill|1831|en|1831|qid=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}} ({{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}} 2).

[[File:Munakata-shrine-torii-chiba-feb21-2016.jpg|thumb|Torii gate]]
== Overview ==
The shrine is regarded as the {{ill|Sōchinju|ja|総鎮守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōchinju|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎮守神|1=Chinjugami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97479976}} (chief shrine) of the many Munakata shrines located around the southern edge of the northern plateau by {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. It is believed to be the oldest shrine among the Munakata shrines in the region. 

Nearby are {{ill|Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|ja|薬王寺 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuō-ji (Shiroi)|qq=}}, associated with local dragon legends, and {{ill|Benzaiten (Shiroi)|ja|弁財天 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten (Shiroi)|qq=}}.

== Access ==
Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|en|Chiba New Town Chūō Station|qid=Q855466|lt=Chiba New Town Chūō Station|1=Chiba New Town Chūō Station}}.
